info.title: Amazon Elastic Beanstalk Tools for .NET Core applications (5.0.1)
info.description:
Project Home: https://github.com/aws/aws-extensions-for-dotnet-cli
Commands to manage environments:
deploy-environment      Deploy the application to an AWS Elastic Beanstalk environment.
list-environments       List the AWS Elastic Beanstalk environments.
delete-environment      Delete an AWS Elastic Beanstalk environment.
Other Commands:
package                 Package the application to a zip file to be deployed later to an Elastic Beanstalk environment
To get help on individual commands execute:
dotnet eb help <command>

options.name: --disable-interactive
options.description: When set to true missing required parameters will not be prompted for.

options.name: --region
options.description: The region to connect to AWS services, if not set region will be detected from the environment.

options.name: --profile
options.description: Profile to use to look up AWS credentials, if not set environment credentials will be used.

options.name: --profile-location
options.description: Optional override to the search location for Profiles, points at a shared credentials file.

options.name: --aws-access-key-id
options.description: The AWS access key id. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-secret-key
options.description: The AWS secret key. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-session-token
options.description: The AWS session token. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --project-location
options.description: The location of the project, if not set the current directory will be assumed.

options.name: --config-file
options.description: Configuration file storing default values for command line arguments.

options.name: --persist-config-file
options.description: If true the arguments used for a successful deployment are persisted to a config file.

options.name: --application
options.description: The name of the Elastic Beanstalk application.

options.name: --environment
options.description: The name of the Elastic Beanstalk environment.

options.name: --disable-interactive
options.description: When set to true missing required parameters will not be prompted for.

options.name: --region
options.description: The region to connect to AWS services, if not set region will be detected from the environment.

options.name: --profile
options.description: Profile to use to look up AWS credentials, if not set environment credentials will be used.

options.name: --profile-location
options.description: Optional override to the search location for Profiles, points at a shared credentials file.

options.name: --aws-access-key-id
options.description: The AWS access key id. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-secret-key
options.description: The AWS secret key. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-session-token
options.description: The AWS session token. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --project-location
options.description: The location of the project, if not set the current directory will be assumed.

options.name: --config-file
options.description: Configuration file storing default values for command line arguments.

options.name: --persist-config-file
options.description: If true the arguments used for a successful deployment are persisted to a config file.

options.name: --configuration
options.description: Configuration to build with, for example Release or Debug.

options.name: --framework
options.description: Target framework to compile, for example netcoreapp3.1.

options.name: --self-contained
options.description: If true a self contained deployment bundle including the targeted .NET runtime will be created.

options.name: --publish-options
options.description: Additional options passed to the "dotnet publish" command.

options.name: --application
options.description: The name of the Elastic Beanstalk application.

options.name: --environment
options.description: The name of the Elastic Beanstalk environment.

options.name: --version-label
options.description: Version label that will be assigned to the uploaded version of code. The default is current tick count.

options.name: --tags
options.description: Tags to assign to the Elastic Beanstalk environment. Format is <tag1>=<value1>;<tag2>=<value2>.

options.name: --app-path
options.description: The application path. The default is '/'.

options.name: --iis-website
options.description: The IIS WebSite for the web application. The default is 'Default Web Site'

options.name: --additional-options
options.description: Additional options for the environment. Format is <option-namespace>,<option-name>=<option-value>;...

options.name: --cname
options.description: CNAME prefix for a new environment.

options.name: --solution-stack
options.description: The type of environment to create. For example "64bit Windows Server 2016 v1.2.0 running IIS 10.0".

options.name: --environment-type
options.description: Type of the environment to launch "LoadBalanced" or "SingleInstance". The default is "LoadBalanced".

options.name: --key-pair
options.description: EC2 Key pair assigned to the EC2 instances for the environment.

options.name: --instance-type
options.description: Type of the EC2 instances launched for the environment. The default is "t2.micro" for Linux and "t3a.medium" for Windows.

options.name: --health-check-url
options.description: Health Check URL.

options.name: --enable-xray
options.description: If set to true then the AWS X-Ray daemon will be enabled on EC2 instances running the application.

options.name: --disable-imds-v1
options.description: If set to true then the IMDSv1 will be disabled on EC2 instances running the application.

options.name: --enhanced-health-type
options.description: The type of enhanced health to be enabled. Valid values: enhanced, basic

options.name: --instance-profile
options.description: Instance profile that provides AWS Credentials to access AWS services.

options.name: --service-role
options.description: IAM role to allow Beanstalk to make calls to AWS services.

options.name: --package
options.description: Application package to use for deployment, skips building the project

options.name: --loadbalancer-type
options.description: LoadBalancer type for the environment. If no value set then a single instance environment type is created. Valid values: application, network, classic

options.name: --enable-sticky-sessions
options.description: If set to true sticky sessions will be enabled for the load balancer of the environment.

options.name: --proxy-server
options.description: The reverse proxy server used on Linux EC2 instances. Valid values: nginx, none. The default is "nginx".

options.name: --application-port
options.description: The application port that will be redirect to port 80. The default is port 5000.

options.name: --wait
options.description: Wait for the environment update to complete before exiting.

options.name: --disable-interactive
options.description: When set to true missing required parameters will not be prompted for.

options.name: --region
options.description: The region to connect to AWS services, if not set region will be detected from the environment.

options.name: --profile
options.description: Profile to use to look up AWS credentials, if not set environment credentials will be used.

options.name: --profile-location
options.description: Optional override to the search location for Profiles, points at a shared credentials file.

options.name: --aws-access-key-id
options.description: The AWS access key id. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-secret-key
options.description: The AWS secret key. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-session-token
options.description: The AWS session token. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --project-location
options.description: The location of the project, if not set the current directory will be assumed.

options.name: --config-file
options.description: Configuration file storing default values for command line arguments.

options.name: --persist-config-file
options.description: If true the arguments used for a successful deployment are persisted to a config file.
